title: Unauthorized File Download
description: Sensitive documents were accessed from a shared workstation. Analyse browsing history and download artefacts to determine the sequence of events.
investigationObjective: Develop skills in correlating authentication artefacts with file access events and recognising the investigative significance of download history as potential exfiltration indicators.

events.description: [Chrome] Visited https://www.reddit.com/r/programming (title: Reddit – r/programming, visit count: 14, last visit: 2024-10-21)
events.explanation: Unrelated programming forum browsing during a break — a regular, habitual visit pattern.

events.description: [Chrome] Google search: "how to pass multiple parameters to React useEffect"
events.explanation: Technical research unrelated to the investigation — a common developer search pattern.

events.description: [Chrome] Bing search: "meridian corp intranet document library" (engine: Bing, SafeSearch: off)
events.explanation: The session pivoted to a targeted Bing search for the internal document repository. The use of Bing (not the browser default) and SafeSearch disabled are notable search behaviour indicators.

relationships.label: The search result led directly to the corporate intranet landing page.
A targeted intranet search commonly precedes SSO session cookie creation.

hints: Think about which action usually happens first when a user wants to reach an internal system.
Users often start from a search before navigating to a known internal portal.
The intranet search should be placed first, before the landing page visit and before the SSO session cookie is set.

events.description: [Chrome] Visited https://intranet.meridiancorp.local/ (title: Meridian Corp Intranet Home, referrer: https://www.bing.com/, IP: 10.0.8.45)
events.explanation: The browser accessed the corporate intranet landing page from the Bing search result. The internal IP address (10.0.8.45) confirms on-premises network access.

relationships.label: Loading an authenticated portal page commonly precedes session cookie creation.

hints: Think about the first destination after someone clicks a Bing search result.
A successful intranet visit frequently leads to authentication artefacts such as session cookies.
The intranet landing page visit should be placed right after the search and before the SSO session cookie appears.

events.description: [Chrome] Cookie set: SSO_SESSION on intranet.meridiancorp.local (Name: SSO_SESSION, Value: mer_sid_9a2f4b8c, Domain: intranet.meridiancorp.local, HttpOnly, Secure, SameSite=None, expires: 2024-10-22T20:33:42Z)
events.explanation: Single sign-on authentication established a session cookie, confirming the user was logged into the intranet. The Secure and SameSite=None flags indicate this cookie supports cross-site requests, relevant for API calls.

relationships.label: Single sign-on authentication typically follows reaching the authenticated portal.
Authenticated sessions often enable subsequent protected internal searches.

hints: Think about which artefact is set once a portal login completes.
Session cookies usually appear after a successful authentication event.
The SSO_SESSION cookie should be placed right after the intranet home page visit and before the internal confidential search.

events.description: [Chrome] Google search: "best lunch spots near city office downtown"
events.explanation: Unrelated personal search for lunch options — a normal midday browsing pattern during a work break.

events.description: [Chrome] Intranet search: "Q3 2024 financial report confidential" (search engine: Meridian Corp Internal Search, results: 3)
events.explanation: An internal search for confidential financial material on the corporate search engine indicates deliberate targeting of sensitive files. The result count of 3 suggests the document was located.

relationships.label: An internal search for sensitive material commonly precedes opening the exact report.

hints: Think about what happens once the user is authenticated and looking for a specific document.
A sensitive file search often reflects a deliberate intent to locate restricted material.
The internal Q3 search should be placed right after the SSO cookie and immediately before the report page is opened.

events.description: [Chrome] Visited https://intranet.meridiancorp.local/finance/reports/q3-2024 (title: Q3 2024 Financial Report, access level: Restricted, referrer: https://intranet.meridiancorp.local/)
events.explanation: The search returned the restricted finance report page in the browser history. The 'access level: Restricted' tag is a key forensic indicator of the document's sensitivity classification.

relationships.label: Opening a report page frequently precedes downloading the document.

hints: Think about which page appears immediately after running a search.
Opening a report page is often the precursor to a local download.
The Q3 report page visit should be placed right after the internal search and before the PDF download occurs.

events.description: [Chrome] Downloaded Q3_2024_Financial_Report_CONFIDENTIAL.pdf to C:\Users\SharedWS\Downloads\ (size: 1.8 MB, browser: Chrome 130.0.6099.130, MIME: application/pdf, etag: "f3a2c1d4-5b6e-7f8a-9c0d-1e2f3a4b5c6d")
events.explanation: The confidential PDF was saved locally, creating a critical exfiltration artefact. The download destination (SharedWS) on a shared workstation is a significant forensic finding — anyone with access to that machine can retrieve the file.

relationships.label: Downloading sensitive material often precedes cloud transfer or sharing activity.

hints: Think about what a user typically does once the report page is open.
A local download can indicate preparation for later transfer or exfiltration.
The confidential PDF download should be placed right after the report page visit and before the cloud storage activity that follows.

events.description: [Chrome] Visited https://mail.google.com/mail/u/0/ (title: Gmail – Inbox, session active)
events.explanation: Unrelated email checking activity — a common personal browsing action that follows normal work patterns and is not relevant to the file download investigation.

events.description: [Chrome] Visited https://drive.google.com/ (title: Google Drive – Home, session active, storage used: 2.4 GB of 15 GB)
events.explanation: Shortly after the download, the user opened Google Drive, suggesting intent to upload or transfer the downloaded file. The storage usage figure confirms an active, established account.

relationships.label: Opening cloud storage shortly after a sensitive download can suggest transfer intent.
Visiting cloud storage commonly precedes cookie refresh during account use.

hints: Think about where someone might take a file shortly after downloading a confidential document.
Opening cloud storage shortly after a sensitive download can suggest transfer intent.
The Google Drive home page visit should be placed right after the PDF download and immediately before the SID cookie refresh.

events.description: [Chrome] Cookie refreshed: SID on .google.com (Name: SID, Domain: .google.com, Value: [REDACTED], Secure, HttpOnly, SameSite=None, SameSite=None requires Secure, expires: 2024-11-21T16:38:51Z)
events.explanation: The Google session cookie was refreshed, confirming the cloud storage account was actively authenticated during the timeframe the user accessed Drive. The SameSite=None and Secure flags indicate the cookie supports cross-site requests — consistent with API usage from Drive.

relationships.label: Cloud storage cookie refresh commonly follows visiting the Drive service.

hints: Think about which cookie activity confirms an active cloud account.
A refreshed Google session cookie suggests active authenticated use of the Drive service.
The Google SID cookie refresh should be placed last, immediately after the Drive home page visit.

events.explanation: Unrelated entertainment browsing after completing the file access sequence — normal break-time activity.
